name: Area-of-Effect Attack
description: Area-of-Effect Attack is an interaction that creates threat, defense, damage, timing, or tactical pressure during conflict.
design_notes: Area-of-Effect Attack should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For combat mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use area-of-effect attack as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect area-of-effect attack to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels powerful, skillful, and able to read danger under pressure.

parameters.name: damage_value
parameters.description: Base amount of damage or threat applied by the mechanic.

parameters.name: startup_time
parameters.description: Delay before the active effect begins.

parameters.name: recovery_time
parameters.description: Delay after use before the actor can fully act again.

edge_cases: Area-of-Effect Attack is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Area-of-Effect Attack overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Area-of-Effect Attack runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Area-of-Effect Attack state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Area-of-Effect Attack plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Area-of-Effect Attack produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Area-of-Effect Attack strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Area-of-Effect Attack becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Area-of-Effect Attack with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Area-of-Effect Attack as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Area-of-Effect Attack with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Area-of-Effect Attack as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
